summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by reading 2 Corinthians 12:7-10, introducing the theme of 'A Thorn in My Flesh.' He explained that a 'thorn' represents a persistent problem, pain, or struggle in life that doesn't seem to go away, such as physical ailments, emotional pain, fear, addiction, or relational difficulties. Pastor Travis emphasized that while these thorns can feel like torment, they are not mistakes; God can use them for a greater purpose. The first point highlighted that there is always a purpose behind our pain, using the example of Paul's thorn, which was allowed to keep him humble. He connected this to the stories of Jacob wrestling with God and the potter's clay from Jeremiah, illustrating that God's blessing and shaping often come through brokenness. The second point focused on God's sufficient grace. Pastor Travis explained that the miracle is not always God removing the thorn but providing His grace to cover, protect, and see us through the difficulty. He defined grace as 'God's Riches At Christ's Expense' and encouraged the congregation that God's grace is enough for every temptation and trial. The third point declared that when we are weak, God is strong. Pastor Travis urged believers to boast in their weaknesses so that Christ's power can rest on them, turning personal hurts into ministry to help others. He concluded by affirming that salvation is a gift of grace through faith in Jesus, who is the answer to every problem, and led the church in a prayer of surrender.
mainPoints: There is always a divine purpose behind the pain and problems (the 'thorn') in our lives, often to cultivate humility and dependence on God.
God's grace is sufficient for every trial; the miracle is not always the removal of the problem but the provision of His covering strength.
In our weakness, God's strength is perfected; we can boast in our weaknesses so that Christ's power can work through us and turn our hurts into ministry.

versesMentioned.text: Therefore, in order to keep me from becoming conceited, I was given a thorn in my flesh, a messenger of Satan, to torment me. Three times I pleaded with the Lord to take it away from me. But he said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ’s power may rest on me. That is why, for Christ’s sake, I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.

No temptation has overtaken you except what is common to mankind. And God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear. But when you are tempted, he will also provide a way out so that you can endure it.
For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sake he became poor, so that you through his poverty might become rich.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
Father, if you are willing, take this cup from me; yet not my will, but yours be done.' An angel from heaven appeared to him and strengthened him.
who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.
For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith—and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God— not by works, so that no one can boast.

Monday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 - 'But he said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness."'
Monday.action: Identify one 'thorn' or struggle you are facing. Instead of asking for its removal today, ask God to show you His sufficient grace in the midst of it.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that your grace is enough for me today. Help me to rely on your strength, not my own, in this situation. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: James 1:2-3 - 'Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance.'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a past difficulty. Write down one way that experience, though painful, helped you grow or depend more on God.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, help me to see your purpose even in hard times. Give me eyes to see how you are maturing my faith. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 1:4 - '...who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.'
Wednesday.action: Think of someone who might be struggling with something you have experienced. Reach out with a text or call to offer encouragement.
Wednesday.prayer: God, use the comfort you've given me to be a comfort to someone else today. Guide my words and actions. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 10:13 - '...God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear. But when you are tempted, he will also provide a way out...'
Thursday.action: Be mindful of a recurring temptation. Before you face it, pray for God to show you the 'way out' He has promised.
Thursday.prayer: Faithful God, I trust that you see my struggles. Please show me your way of escape and give me the strength to take it. Amen.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 73:26 - 'My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.'
Friday.action: In an area where you feel weak or inadequate today, speak this truth aloud: 'When I am weak, then I am strong in Christ.'
Friday.prayer: Lord, I admit my weakness. I boast in it today, so that your power may be seen in my life. Be my strength. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: Ephesians 2:8 - 'For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith—and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God—'
Saturday.action: Take a moment of quiet gratitude. Thank God that your relationship with Him is based on His gift of grace, not your own performance.
Saturday.prayer: Thank you, Jesus, for the free gift of salvation. I receive your grace anew today and rest in your finished work. Amen.
